In terms of vulnerability types, the top three vulnerabilities that caused the most losses in the first three quarters of 2024 were business logic flaws, reentrancy vulnerabilities, and access control vulnerabilities, accounting for 34.7%, 34.6%, and 10%, respectively. The most frequently occurring vulnerabilities are also business logic vulnerabilities, followed by validation issues.

Figure 8 The proportion of security incidents with different hacker attack methods in 2024

Data: Footprint Analytics, @Beosin, 2024.Q1-2024.Q3

3.3 Analysis of attacked project types

From the perspective of project track, in the first three quarters of 2024, centralized exchanges (CEX) suffered the most serious losses, accounting for as high as 35.8%, with cumulative losses reaching US$688 million. Among them, the DMM Bitcoin incident was the most serious, with a loss of up to $308 million. It is the seventh largest security incident in the history of cryptocurrency hacker attacks and the security incident with the largest loss in 2024. The DMM Bitcoin incident is the third largest cryptocurrency exchange theft in Japan after the Mt.Gox incident in 2014 and the Coincheck incident in 2018. Because CEX concentrates a large number of user assets, it is easier to become a key target for hackers. Although the frequency of security incidents in CEX is relatively low, the losses caused by a single incident are often huge, posing a great threat to the security of the entire exchange ecosystem.

Secondly, wallets and game projects also suffered large losses, accounting for 21.8% and 20.2% respectively. Wallets are the first choice for users to store encrypted assets. Once they are hacked, the losses are often heavy. Game projects have also become high-risk areas for hacker attacks due to their large user base and a large number of virtual asset transactions. Take the attack on Gala Games on May 20 as an example. The attacker minted a large number of tokens and then quickly exchanged them for other mainstream cryptocurrencies, causing huge losses to the platform.

Figure 9: Proportion of loss amount of attacked project types in crypto asset security incidents in 2024

Data: Footprint Analytics, @Beosin; 2024.Q1-2024.Q3

From the number of attacked projects, DeFi is the most vulnerable field. According to Beosin Alert data, in the first three quarters of 2024, the number of attacks on DeFi projects accounted for as high as 45.5%, becoming the main target of hackers. The complexity of the DeFi protocol, the high concentration of funds, and the frequent security vulnerabilities are the main reasons for its repeated attacks. In contrast, although centralized exchanges (CEX) and wallet projects have also been attacked, the number of attacks is relatively small due to the use of multiple security measures. However, DeFi projects are attacked most frequently, but due to the relatively small amount of a single transaction, the direct economic losses caused may be lower than CEX. This is because CEX stores a large amount of user assets, and once it is breached, the losses caused are often more serious.

Figure 10. Percentage of attacked projects in crypto asset security incidents in 2024

Data: Footprint Analytics, @Beosin, 2024.Q1-2024.Q3

3.4 Analysis of attacked ecosystem

From the perspective of attacked ecosystem, in 2024, Ethereum is still the public chain with the highest loss, reaching US$460 million; followed by BSC, with a loss of approximately US$86.08 million, followed by Arbitrum, with approximately US$83.23 100 million US dollars. Ethereum has become the preferred target of hacker attacks mainly because it is currently the largest smart contract platform with a rich ecosystem and a huge amount of funds. BSC, as a competitor of Ethereum, has also faced a large number of attacks, and the amount of losses is second only to Ethereum.

It is worth noting that the rapid development of the Solana ecosystem in 2024 has also made it the focus of hackers. For example, on May 16, the Solana-based token launcher pump.fun suffered an attack using flash loans, with losses of up to 80 million US dollars. This incident highlights that the Solana ecosystem still has great challenges in security.

In addition, with the rise of Layer2 solutions such as Arbitrum and Optimism, the security of these ecosystems has also received increasing attention. Although these ecosystems have made many technical optimizations, they are still inevitably subject to hacker attacks.

Figure 11 Number of security incidents and loss amount of crypto assets in each ecosystem in 2024

Data: SlowMist Hacked, 2024.01 - 2024.11

In addition, by comparing the loss amount and the proportion of the number of incidents of crypto asset security incidents in each ecosystem in 2024, we can find that the loss amount of the Ethereum ecosystem accounts for as high as 62.6%, which is much higher than other ecosystems. Although the number of security incidents accounts for only 39%, the loss caused by a single attack is much higher than that of other ecosystems. This may be related to its status as the largest smart contract platform, with a rich DeFi ecosystem and a huge amount of locked funds. Once attacked, the losses are often more severe. In contrast, the number of security incidents in the BSC ecosystem is comparable to that of Ethereum, accounting for 32%, but the amount of losses accounts for only 11.7%, indicating that although the incidents are frequent, the losses caused by a single attack are relatively small.

Figure 12: The proportion of security incidents and losses of each ecological crypto asset in 2024

Data: SlowMist Hacked, 2024.01 - 2024.11

Figure 13: The proportion of security incidents of each ecological crypto asset in 2024

Data: SlowMist Hacked, 2024.01 - 2024.11

3.5 Review of Attacks in 2024

In 2024, the cryptocurrency industry faced a severe security situation, and hacker attacks occurred frequently, causing huge economic losses to the industry. The following summarizes some major security incidents in the first three quarters of 2024, covering different attack methods and significant loss amounts.

Figure 14: Some typical attacks on encryption security in 2024

Data: SlowMist Hacked

4. Funds flow of encryption security incidents in 2024

4.1 Analysis of the flow of stolen funds

According to Beosin KYT data, about 25.3% (4.86%) of the funds stolen in 2024 were stolen. In 2020, about 10.9% (US$209 million) of the stolen funds were transferred to exchanges, a higher proportion than in 2023. Only 5.1% (about US$98 million) were transferred to mixers, and the amount of stolen funds cleaned through mixers has been greatly reduced.

Figure 15 Funds flow of crypto security incidents in 2024 (million US dollars)

Data: Footprint Analytics, @Beosin, 2024.Q1-2024.Q3

From the above data, it can be seen that there are four main destinations for hackers to steal funds: frozen or recovered, kept in the hacker's address, transferred to the exchange, and cleaned through the mixer. Among them, Tornado Cash is one of the most commonly used mixers. It allows users to improve transaction privacy through mixed transactions, but it may also be used for illegal activities such as money laundering. Beosin KYT data shows that in the first half of 2024, the amount of money laundered by hackers using Tornado Cash increased significantly compared with 2023, an increase of 15.42% compared with the first half of the year and 103.42% compared with the second half of the year, indicating that hackers are increasingly relying on Tornado Cash to conceal the source of their funds.

Figure 16 Amount of funds stolen by hackers flowing to Tornado Cash (million US dollars)

Data: Footprint Analytics, @Beosin, 2024.Q1-2024.Q3

As criminals use mixers such as Tornado Cash to launder money, regulators are paying more and more attention to cryptocurrency mixing services. In August 2022, the U.S. Treasury Department's move to impose sanctions on Tornado Cash marked the regulator's tough attitude towards the balance between cryptocurrency privacy and anti-money laundering. This incident has attracted widespread attention from the entire industry, and compliance and risk management have become the top priorities for crypto platforms. Governments around the world have stepped up their supervision of cryptocurrency mixing services to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing activities.

4.2 Money laundering methods for stolen funds

In recent years, the money laundering methods for stolen cryptocurrency funds have become increasingly complex and diverse. Hackers continue to innovate and try their best to conceal the source of funds through multi-layer transfers, mixing services, DEX transactions, and anonymous coins. Among them, the North Korean hacker group Lazarus Group is particularly active, launching cyber attacks on financial institutions and cryptocurrency exchanges many times, causing huge losses. For example, the Axie Infinity Ronin attack and the DMM Bitcoin attack are closely related to the Lazarus Group, and both incidents are among the largest attacks in the history of cryptocurrency.

The money laundering strategy of the Lazarus Group has evolved over the years and has formed a mature and complex system. They usually take the following steps:

1. Initial obfuscation: Deposit the stolen cryptocurrency into a mixer such as Tornado Cash to cut off the transaction chain and achieve initial anonymization.

2. Cross-chain transfer: Use cross-chain protocols such as Thorchain to convert funds into different cryptocurrencies to increase the difficulty of tracing.

3. Fund obfuscation: Lazarus Group obfuscated funds through multiple addresses. For example, part of the funds were transferred to the Bitcoin chain through a cross-chain, and then the tBTC protocol was used to transfer the funds to Ethereum, further increasing the complexity of money laundering.

4. Distributed storage: Distribute the funds to multiple addresses and transfer them to chains with less supervision, such as the TRON chain.

5. Over-the-counter transactions: Conduct over-the-counter transactions through platforms such as Paxful and Noones to convert crypto assets into fiat currency or other cryptocurrencies to circumvent KYC review.

Industry analysts generally believe that the inflow of funds from Lazarus Group to Tornado Cash is closely related, indicating that Tornado Cash plays an indispensable role in hacker money laundering activities. Data shows that the amount of ETH deposited by Lazarus Group through Tornado.Cash has shown a fluctuating upward trend, indicating that its money laundering activities continue to be active. Although regulators continue to strengthen supervision of Tornado Cash, Lazarus Group has successfully circumvented supervision by continuously innovating money laundering methods, such as multi-layer transfers and cross-chain transfers, which has increased the difficulty of law enforcement. In response to the money laundering activities of hacker organizations, regulators need to keep pace with the times and strengthen international cooperation to more effectively combat cryptocurrency crimes.

Figure 17. Amount of money deposited by Lazarus Group in Tornado Cash

Data: DUNE, @tornado_cash

4.3 Tracking the whereabouts of funds for crypto security cases in 2024

4.3.1 Tracking the stolen funds from DMM Bitcoin: suspected to be the work of Lazarus Group

4.3.1.1 Background introduction

On May 31, 2024, hackers hacked into the DMM Bitcoin platform and stole 4,502.9 Bitcoins, worth approximately US$308 million. As of December 2, the value of these stolen Bitcoins had risen to more than US$429 million. After the incident, DMM Bitcoin restricted withdrawals and cryptocurrency purchases on the platform to mitigate losses. However, these measures failed to prevent the expansion of losses, but instead had a negative impact on user services.

4.3.1.2 Funding Path

Blockchain security experts found that the stolen bitcoins were quickly dispersed to multiple wallets and laundered through suspicious platforms such as Huione Guarantee. The attack method and money laundering model are highly suspected to be the work of the North Korean state-backed hacker organization Lazarus Group.

Beosin Trace's tracking shows that the stolen 4,502.9 bitcoins have been dispersed to 10 new addresses. Blockchain detective ZachXBT's investigation shows that Lazarus Group has laundered more than $35 million of DMM Bitcoin stolen funds through Huione Guarantee in Cambodia.

Figure 18 DMM Bitcoin stolen funds path

Data: BEOSIN

4.3.1.3 Regulatory challenges

This incident has aroused widespread market concern about the security of cryptocurrency exchanges. The suspension of DMM Bitcoin highlights the severe security challenges faced by exchanges and has attracted close attention from regulators. The results of the investigation by the Financial Services Agency (FSA) of Japan showed that the company had serious deficiencies in risk management, including the lack of independent audits, the centralization of security functions, and violations of cryptocurrency trading regulations.

The investigation found that DMM Bitcoin had not established a sound risk management system, and its internal audit was non-existent, which failed to effectively prevent the loss of crypto assets. The company did not assign a dedicated person to be responsible for risk management, and the relevant responsibilities were concentrated in the hands of a few people. In addition, the company did not keep key logs that would help investigate the theft, which violated relevant regulations. The FSA has issued a "business improvement order" to the company and emphasized that the company has serious problems in system risk management and responding to the risk of crypto asset leakage.

This incident is one of the most important cryptocurrency thefts in 2024 and the second largest illegal cryptocurrency outflow in Japanese history. It highlights the escalating cybersecurity threats in the digital asset field and has aroused widespread concern about the regulation of cryptocurrency exchanges. The experience of DMM Bitcoin once again reminds us that cryptocurrency exchanges are facing huge security risks. In order to protect user assets, exchanges must continuously strengthen security measures. At the same time, regulators should also strengthen supervision of the cryptocurrency market to maintain market order and prevent similar incidents from happening again.

4.3.2 Turkish Crypto Ponzi Scheme: Stolen Funds Tracking

4.3.2.1 Background

On May 30, 2024, Turkish police conducted a large-scale raid on a cryptocurrency project called Smart Trade Coin (STC), arrested 127 suspects of fraud, and seized a large number of assets and guns.

Since its launch in 2021, the STC project has attracted a large number of Turkish investors with its promise of high returns that can connect multiple cryptocurrency exchanges and manage multiple trading accounts in a unified manner. However, as time goes by, more and more investors begin to suspect that the project is a Ponzi scheme. Victims' lawyers said that up to 50,000 Turkish investors are deeply involved, and the total loss amount may exceed US$2 billion. Many users reported losing 95% of their savings and were unable to verify whether these funds were misappropriated by the STC team.

4.3.2.2 Funding Path

Beosin KYT's on-chain fund tracking analysis based on the project name Smart Trade Coin shows that the STC token contract transferred most of the funds through the 0x5f45 address and eventually flowed into the 0xc12c address. Further tracking found that the 0xc12c address conducted a large number of one-way ETH transfer transactions, with a huge amount of transfers, close to the published estimated loss amount. Moreover, all transaction fees involving ETH transfers were paid by the 0xc12c address, which further proves that the address was used to distribute stolen funds.

The figure below only shows part of the fund flow, and the 0xc12c address involved more than 20,000 transfer transactions. According to the tracked transaction data, after the stolen funds were distributed, part of them were directly transferred to major exchanges, while the other part eventually flowed into the exchanges through complex operations such as splitting, merging, and obfuscation.

Figure 19 Smart Trade Coin on-chain fund path

Data: BEOSIN

4.3.2.3 Regulatory challenges

This incident highlights the serious lack of regulation in the Turkish cryptocurrency market. Although the government has been encouraging innovation, the lack of an effective regulatory framework has allowed criminals to take advantage of the situation and harmed the interests of the majority of investors. Local governments should establish a sound regulatory system as soon as possible to protect the rights and interests of investors and promote the healthy development of the cryptocurrency industry.

Turkey's experience shows that it is not advisable to simply pursue cryptocurrency freedom. While encouraging innovation and development, it is necessary to strengthen supervision and establish a compliant and transparent market environment. Only in this way can cryptocurrency truly play its value and become an effective tool to promote economic development and hedge risks. The government and the industry should work together to formulate sound regulatory laws and regulations, strengthen market supervision, improve industry transparency, and jointly create a safe and reliable cryptocurrency investment environment.

5. Anti-money laundering supervision of crypto security incidents

Money laundering activities in the cryptocurrency field are intensifying, posing a serious threat to financial security. Since 2024, in response to this challenge, the global regulatory efforts on cryptocurrencies have been continuously strengthened. Regulators in various countries require virtual asset service providers to strengthen KYC/AML compliance and actively participate in international regulatory cooperation. However, how to protect the interests of investors without stifling innovation is a major problem facing regulators. The cryptocurrency industry also needs to actively adapt to the regulatory environment and seek a balance between compliance and business development.

Countries have shown diversity in their practices in anti-money laundering regulation. Taking Hong Kong, Singapore, the United States, Europe, Japan, Canada, Australia, South Korea, Turkey and Malaysia as examples, these regions have all introduced corresponding regulatory policies, mainly focusing on the following aspects: First, strengthen the supervision of virtual asset trading platforms and require relevant licenses; second, strengthen anti-money laundering and anti-terrorist financing measures, such as implementing the Travel Rule (fund movement rules: This rule requires financial institutions that handle the transfer of encrypted assets to pass customer information to the next institution, which should include the name and address of the sender and recipient), and strengthen KYC certification; third, pay attention to the supervision of stablecoins, requiring improved transparency and capital reserves; fourth, protect the rights and interests of investors and combat fraud and cybercrime. These regulatory measures show that a consensus is forming globally that the supervision of the cryptocurrency market needs to be strengthened to maintain financial stability and protect the interests of investors.

Figure 20 Anti-money laundering regulatory measures for cryptocurrency in various countries

6. Conclusion

In 2024, the security situation of crypto assets remains severe. Hacker attack methods are constantly updated, which brings significant challenges to the development of the industry. Although old problems such as rug pull, smart contract vulnerabilities and private key leaks still exist, the weak security awareness of users and the emergence of new attack methods have made the security protection of crypto assets more complicated. A number of major security incidents have revealed the vulnerability of decentralized exchanges and other platforms in asset protection, highlighting the urgent need to strengthen security measures.

Security incidents such as the DMM Bitcoin incident and the Turkish crypto Ponzi scheme sounded the alarm, forcing regulators in various countries to accelerate the pace of regulation of the cryptocurrency market. By strengthening anti-money laundering and KYC measures, regulators aim to protect the rights and interests of investors, combat financial crimes, and maintain market stability. At present, countries around the world have taken measures such as issuing licenses, strengthening anti-money laundering, protecting investors, and regulating stablecoins. For example, Hong Kong, China, has introduced a virtual asset OTC licensing system, and Singapore has strengthened supervision of digital payment token service providers. The US SEC has strengthened supervision of crypto lending products, and Europe has passed the Market Crypto Assets Regulation (MiCA), which provides a unified regulatory standard for the cryptocurrency market. These regulatory measures are aimed at balancing innovation and risk, and building a safer, more transparent and compliant ecosystem for the cryptocurrency industry.

In the future, the cryptocurrency industry will still need to find a balance between innovation and security. Only by improving technical means, strengthening security protection and improving the legal framework can we effectively deal with increasingly complex cyber threats. In addition, international cooperation between regulatory authorities of various countries is crucial, which will promote information sharing and coordinated regulatory strategies, and jointly build a safer and more transparent cryptocurrency ecosystem. Only in this way can we achieve long-term sustainable development and create a safer investment environment for investors.

data.title: Trump's trade war: Why isn't Wall Street worried?
data.description:
Investors seem to think that Trump's tariffs are "much ado about nothing," but U.S. stocks are not immune to further shocks from tariffs or other policy proposals, and Trump may be more emboldened to test extreme measures because of the relatively calm stock market reaction.

The global trade war does not seem to scare Wall Street.

On Thursday (February 13th) local time, U.S. President Trump announced that he would explore how to impose reciprocal tariffs on U.S. trading partners, which is another major factor that may lead to the gradual escalation of the global trade war he launched.

However, the S&P 500 index closed up 1% at 6,110 points on the day, close to the historical high of 6,118.71 points set on January 23. The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed up 0.8%, and the Nasdaq Composite Index closed up 1.5%.

Investors seem to think the tariffs are "more noise than rain," said George Young, partner and portfolio manager at investment advisory firm Villere & Co.

Stocks rose because the executive order did not immediately lead to tariffs as investors and foreign leaders feared, and other tariff-related actions were considered less severe than initially proposed.

Earlier this week, Trump announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imported into the United States. Earlier in February, Trump announced a 25% tariff on Canadian and Mexican goods and a 10% tariff on Chinese goods. Trump quickly pressed the "pause button" after Canadian and Mexican leaders pledged to take steps to strengthen border enforcement and combat drug trafficking.

But Yang believes that the rise in the stock market does not mean that the market is immune to further shocks from tariff measures or other policy proposals.

He said: "We know that this has become a new normal. The stock market has not gotten rid of the problem of tariffs, but is absorbing it little by little while waiting to see what kind of policies will be implemented."

Indeed, although stock market bulls are jumping for joy, the tariff issue is far from being resolved. Senior White House officials told reporters that reciprocal tariffs may take effect in a few weeks or months, and the US government will first review the countries with the largest trade surpluses with the United States.

Paul Ashworth, chief North American economist at Capital Economics, pointed out that the Trump administration seems to have abandoned its call for a general tariff of 10% to 20% on imported goods, but the broad criteria set by officials to evaluate new tariffs may lead to larger increases in average tariffs and US consumer prices.

Matt Eagan, portfolio manager at Loomis, Sayles & Company, believes the tariff threat is one of "many paradoxes" embedded in Trump's agenda that are difficult to unravel and could cause stock markets to fluctuate wildly from time to time.

Eagan wrote in a research note, "Extending tax cuts may stimulate consumption, but it will also lead to a worsening of the fiscal deficit; immigration controls may tighten the labor market, but they will also lead to higher wages; tariffs may trigger a trade war while suppressing demand and raising prices. We believe that investors should look through the noise and focus on Trump's goals and constraints. His actions may be 'more noise than rain', but we advise investors not to become complacent."

Christopher Smart, managing partner of geopolitical risk consulting firm Arbroath Group, warned that investor complacency could become part of a dangerous feedback loop. "It's hard to know what tariffs will ultimately be implemented, or how high they will be, but it's a safe bet that a lot of them will go into effect, and at the same time, given the relatively muted reaction from investors so far, Trump may be emboldened to test extremes," Smart said.

data.title: The $30 trillion RWA market is booming, and Chinese companies are already investing
data.description:
In 2024, RWA will rise globally, with a market size of more than 14 billion US dollars. But in the eyes of many industry insiders and researchers, 2024 is just an appetizer, and 2025 will be the real first year of RWA explosion.

Before this, internationally renowned institutions such as Citibank, McKinsey, and BCG have made predictions on the potential scale of RWA. The minimum prediction is that by 2030, there will be 4 trillion US dollars, and the maximum will exceed 30 trillion US dollars.

For 2025, the investment institution Bitwise predicts that with the accelerated entry of Wall Street institutions, the RWA market size is expected to reach 50 billion US dollars, and it is likely to grow exponentially from then on.

In 2024, some landmark events have occurred in the RWA field. For example, Tether, the largest stablecoin issuer, and Visa, the largest card organization, have launched their own RWA platforms.

Two important things also happened in the Chinese mainland market:

First, mainland assets successfully issued RWA overseas. Ant Digital cooperated with listed companies Langxin Technology and GCL Energy to issue 100 million and 200 million RWA overseas respectively.

Second, the Shanghai Data Exchange issued the first RWA with the landmark agricultural product Malu Grape data asset as the underlying asset, and all the processes were completed in the mainland.

These two things show that RWA is not only a matter of the overseas crypto market, but also a major opportunity for mainland enterprises and investment institutions.

The full name of RWA is Real World Assets-tokenization, which refers to the conversion of the relevant rights of traditional financial assets and physical assets into digital tokens on the blockchain. These digital tokens represent the ownership, income rights or other agreed rights of traditional financial assets or physical assets.

It closely links the cryptocurrency market with real-world assets. This connection is not a simple grafting and superposition, but promotes the iteration of business models. RWA is still in its early stages of development. We initially observe the business model changes brought about by RWA from three levels: RWA's promotion of the monetization of traditional assets, RWA's "re-financialization" in the crypto market, and RWA's reverse transformation of the real-world business model.

 01 The first level -The change in the monetization model and depth of traditional assets

"Real-world assets" have a wide range of meanings, including: various financial assets in the traditional financial market, such as bonds, stocks, funds, bills, etc.; various identifiable physical assets, such as real estate, land, goods, etc.; various cultural assets, such as calligraphy and painting, antiques, music, film and television works, etc. Regardless of the type of asset, the improvement of liquidity will further enhance its financial attributes and liquidity, thereby gaining recognition and trading opportunities on a larger scale.

These "real world assets" have different degrees of acceptance from niche to mass in the "real world", and also have different degrees of liquidity due to the financial system and regulatory rules of the "real world", that is, their "monetization" levels are different.

RWA, in the form of Token, has changed the mode of monetization of real world assets, strengthened the depth of monetization, and improved the level of monetization. It is mainly reflected in the following aspects:

Improve transaction efficiency:RWA is a 7×24 hours×365 days endless T+0 transaction. Even highly liquid assets in "real world assets", such as government bonds, stocks, funds, etc., can only be traded during limited trading hours on weekdays, and T+0 cannot be achieved in many countries and regions. RWA improves the liquidity of assets and the efficiency of capital use. For assets with low liquidity, after tokenization, their monetization level has been greatly improved. In addition, RWA is more convenient for cross-regional transactions than the "real world".

Lower the entry threshold:RWA allows assets that originally required high thresholds to be "universalized" and allows more investors to participate. According to the original rules of cryptocurrency, there is no permission and no threshold, but in the actual operation of RWA, some projects still retain certain thresholds, but they are much lower than the thresholds in the "real world".

A typical example is the RWA of private equity funds. In the "real world", the investment threshold of private equity funds is high, and its investors have long been limited to a few institutional investors and high-net-worth investors. At the same time, liquidity is also poor. Private equity investment company Hamilton Lane tokenized part of its fund shares on the Polygon network and opened it to investors on the trading platform Securitize. The minimum investment threshold has been greatly reduced from an average of $5 million to only $20,000 (private equity fund KKR has reduced the threshold to $100,000 in similar products), but individual investors still need to pass the qualified investor verification of the Securitize platform, and there is still a certain threshold. However, tokenization enables individual investors to participate in private equity investment in a digital way.

Increase the user base:On the one hand, the recipients, investors and users of crypto assets are younger overall; on the other hand, the cyberpunk cultural group is a loyal user of crypto assets, and this group is constantly expanding. During the campaign, US President Trump changed his past critical attitude towards cryptocurrencies and turned to strongly support cryptocurrencies, partly due to the rapid growth of this group, and supporting cryptocurrencies is conducive to winning this part of the votes. RWA transforms traditional assets in the "real world" into crypto assets, which is conducive to increasing the user base. According to data provided by JunGu Digital Technology, in January 2025, the total number of fans of the official Twitter (now X) of active web3 projects has exceeded 780 million, while this figure was 300 million in 2019.

Save operating costs:Although RWA has a high operating cost in the early stages of development due to the construction of a new system, RWA will gradually reduce costs compared to the financial operating costs of the "real world": First, smart contracts can reduce the operating costs of multi-party transaction processes, reduce human intervention and operational risks, and improve transaction efficiency; second, with the continuous iteration of blockchain technology, the processing speed will increase, and the transaction cost will continue to decline; third, as the scale of RWA grows, the unit operating cost will be greatly reduced.

Improve transparency:For non-standardized bulk trading commodities, a major obstacle to liquidity in "real world" transactions is information transparency and trust. RWA uses blockchain technology to realize a "trustless" information sharing mechanism by putting data and transactions on the chain, which improves transparency and can better ensure transaction fairness.

Monetization of new production factors:As a new production factor, monetization of data is a very important condition for it to play its factor value. RWA provides a new way to monetize data elements. For example, the Malu Grape RWA project of the Shanghai Data Exchange has realized market-oriented transactions through the tokenization of data assets, which has enhanced the commercial value of data.

"Malu Grape" is one of the most representative agricultural product brands in Shanghai and has obtained the national geographical indication certification trademark. In combination with the development of the "Malu Grape" industrial chain, the Shanghai Data Exchange has joined hands with the Malu Town Economic Development Service Center, Zuo'an Xinhui (Shanghai) Data Technology Co., Ltd., "Malu Grape" planting enterprises, as well as infrastructure service providers, information security service providers, law firms, accounting firms, asset appraisal companies and other digital companies to explore innovative applications of data elements. As the project operator, Zuo'an Xinhui (Shanghai) Data Technology Co., Ltd. integrates agricultural products and agricultural product production data into project assets based on the core architecture of the SwiftLink management platform, and relies on the tradable and tradable attributes of the project operator's shares to ensure the accuracy, completeness and timeliness of information disclosure, enhance investor confidence, and complete 10 million yuan of equity financing.

 02 The second level - "re-financialization" of RWA

RWA has transformed the monetization model of "real world assets" and generated new crypto assets. As a crypto asset, RWA has become the starting point of a new business model - "re-financialization", that is, using RWA as the underlying asset to continue to derive new financial models. In the early stages of RWA development, at least two models have been explored.

First, the combination with stablecoins.

Currently, stablecoins can be mainly divided into the following categories:

Fiat-collateralized stablecoins are collateralized by fiat currencies (such as the US dollar, the euro, etc.), usually issued at a ratio of 1:1, such as USDT (Tether), USDC (USD Coin), etc.; Cryptocurrency-collateralized stablecoins are collateralized by cryptocurrencies (such as BTC, ETH, etc.), usually over-collateralized to cope with price fluctuations, and a typical representative is DAI (MakerDAO); Algorithmic stablecoins maintain currency value stability by adjusting market supply and demand through algorithms, with no collateral or partial collateral, such as UST (formerly TerraUSD), AMPL (Ampleforth), etc.; Hybrid stablecoins combine fiat-collateralized stablecoins, cryptocurrency-collateralized stablecoins and algorithmic mechanisms; Commodity-collateralized stablecoins are collateralized by physical assets such as gold and oil, such as PAX Gold (PAXG), etc.

Fiat-collateralized stablecoins can also be said to be a type of RWA, which is an RWA of fiat currency and its equivalents. For example, more than 80% of USDT's collateral is US dollars and its cash equivalents, of which about 80% is US Treasury bonds. To a certain extent, USDT can be said to be the RWA of the US dollar or US Treasury bonds.

The development of RWA provides a broader idea for stablecoins. Because the underlying of RWA is "real-world assets", it can form a "consensus" among a wider group of people, and the price fluctuations of the underlying assets are easier to predict, so RWA is likely to become a collateral asset for issuing stablecoins. The diversity of RWA's underlying assets provides the possibility for diverse stablecoins, and there is an opportunity to meet the needs of different types of users.

Tether, the world's largest stablecoin USDT issuer, has already taken action. On November 14, 2024, Tether announced the launch of a new RWA tokenization platform, Harden by Tether, which aims to simplify the tokenization process of all assets from stocks, bonds, stablecoins to loyalty points, and open up new opportunities for individuals, companies, and even cyber countries to raise funds using tokenized collateral.

In November 2024, Usual, a decentralized legal stablecoin issuer, went online. It plans to integrate the emerging RWAs into a permissionless, on-chain verifiable and composable stablecoin USD0. USD0 is backed by RWA at an ultra-short term of 1: 1, ensuring its stability and security. USD0, as an aggregated RWA stablecoin, can be minted on Usual in two different ways: one is direct RWA deposits, where users deposit eligible RWAs into the protocol and receive an equivalent amount of USD0 at a ratio of 1: 1; the other is indirect USDC/USDT deposits, where users deposit USDC/USDT into the protocol and receive USD0 at a ratio of 1: 1. This indirect method involves third-party collateral providers who provide the necessary RWA collateral, allowing users to obtain USD0 without directly handling RWA.

Second, while combining stablecoins, RWA combines with DeFi (decentralized finance) to create more diversified business models and application scenarios.

In this regard, MakerDao has made many attempts. MakerDao is one of the largest DeFi applications at present. It is an Ethereum-based (DeFi) protocol whose main function is to issue and manage decentralized stablecoin DAI. DAI maintains a 1:1 peg to the US dollar by over-collateralizing cryptocurrencies such as ETH. Users deposit crypto assets into smart contracts to generate DAI for trading or lending. MakerDAO ensures system stability by dynamically adjusting the collateral rate, liquidation mechanism, and community governance (MKR token holders voting).

In September 2020, MakerDAO began to try to use real estate project tokens as collateral to generate DAI (stablecoin). MakerDAO has partnered with Centrifuge to help 6sCapital, a real estate development secured loan project, tokenize and accept the real estate development secured loan token as part of the DAI collateral. In May 2022, MakerDAO launched the Endgame plan, explicitly stating that incorporating RWA into the pledge framework is a key move in building a reserve asset portfolio. According to MakerBurn, as of July 4, 2024, there are 11 RWA projects with a total of more than $3 billion in RWA tokens that can be used as collateral for DAI, and RWA accounts for about 37% of MakerDAO's total assets.

In terms of lending, in April 2021, MakerDAO began issuing DAI loans with RWA tokens as collateral to New Silver, a real estate investor loan service provider, and opened a $5 million credit line. For NewSilver, this cooperation allows it to raise funds more easily and quickly by acquiring DAI and conduct related businesses efficiently.

MakerDAO's cooperation with Centrifuge combines stablecoins with DeFi. Centrifuge is a decentralized asset financing protocol that unlocks the liquidity of real-world assets, allowing borrowers to finance their real-world assets without banks or other intermediaries. Centrifuge connects real-world assets to DeFi, and MakerDAO has become a stable source of funds for 80% of Centrifuge's total active loans, forming a positive cycle between the two: MakerDAO obtains high-quality collateral assets through Centrifuge; and its financial support drives Centrifuge's business growth.

In addition to working with MakerDAO, Centrifuge has also established cooperation with other DeFi projects. It has teamed up with leading DeFi such as Aave to build an RWA-exclusive financing pool, laying the foundation for rapid user aggregation and anchoring liquidity.

With the improvement of technical details, the exploration of business models, and the improvement of regulatory frameworks, bank credit and DeFi lending protocols may be interoperable through RWA, and RWA will make the business model of the financial industry more diverse and the ecology richer.

 03 The third level -RWA's reshaping of the real world business model

RWA's truly disruptive change is to change the real world's business model by linking the real world and the crypto market and building a value bridge. The essence of this change is to transform the "static possession of ownership" in traditional business into "dynamic participation in value flow". At the same time, the RWA "value flow" model will guide the real world to change when "value is produced" to meet the requirements of RWA. At present, at least two possibilities can be observed:

First, gradually shift from the "on-chain model" to the "on-chain model". As RWA gradually achieves remarkable success in the world of crypto assets, more and more companies hope to integrate existing assets to meet the requirements of "on-chain". At the same time, more companies will start on the blockchain when building new assets, and even more and more teams will start businesses on the chain from the beginning. Just like when Taobao first started e-commerce, it attracted small and medium-sized enterprises to open stores online. As Taobao grew, more and more people opened stores directly online and designed their own stores completely according to the Internet model. As a result, the entire supply chain was rapidly changing according to the network and user needs.

For example, in order to realize the cross-border issuance of RWA by mainland assets (which may also involve other crypto applications in the future), Ant Digital has built two chains and one bridge: "asset chain", "transaction chain" and "Ant Chain Trusted Cross-Chain Bridge". The application of "asset chain" in the mainland enables the physical assets of enterprises to be digitized and standardized, and thus transformed into tradable financial products. "Transaction chain" focuses on tokenizing funds, especially funds from traditional financial institutions, to achieve efficient circulation and transactions of funds through blockchain technology. Ant Digital has built a large-scale blockchain open architecture HOU, which supports 100,000-level transaction TPS performance, second-level transaction on-chain time, and PB-level data ledger storage and processing capabilities.

It can be guessed that the business model that Ant Digital may build in the RWA field is to gradually standardize the two chains and one bridge and turn it into infrastructure. In the future, enterprises only need to build assets or even the entire enterprise on Ant's chain according to the needs of the crypto market. As a result, Ant Digital has achieved a business model upgrade, and the enterprises on these chains will no longer operate according to today's business model.

Second, DAO (decentralized organization) business is becoming more and more popular. There are already many DAO business organizations in the overseas market, such as the aforementioned DeFi institution MakerDAO, but they are basically pure cryptocurrency businesses. With the development of RWA, these institutions have a deep linkage with real-world businesses. As the interaction and connection between the real world and the crypto market become more and more in-depth, there will be real-world business models operating in the form of DAOs, and there will be more and more of them, and the proportion will be higher and higher.

 04 Preliminary observation of three types of RWA

RWA can be divided into three categories at present from the aspects of operation mode and regulatory environment. One is RWA operated on overseas public chains, the second is RWA issued cross-border by assets in China (currently only two orders announced by Ant Digits, Longxin Group and GCL Energy), and the third is RWA exploration in China (currently only the Malu Grape RWA of Shanghai Data Exchange has been announced). There are significant differences among the three.

First, overseas RWAs are basically carried out on public chains, publicly issued and financed on the chain, and the sources of funds include both public investors and institutional investors.

Second, the cross-border RWA currently operated by Ant Digital Technology has a domestic "asset chain" that is a consortium chain, and cooperates with public chains overseas. However, it has not yet announced the issuance of tokens for public investors on the public chain. Currently, the funds should mainly come from institutional investors.

Third, the Shanghai Digital Exchange's Malu Grape RWA project operations are all carried out on the domestic consortium chain, and the fundraising carrier is the equity of the data company Zuo'an Xinhui. The issuance and circulation methods of its RWA tokens have not yet been disclosed.

Fourth, the cooperation between overseas RWA and cryptocurrency fields such as DeFi is becoming more and more in-depth, and the methods are becoming more and more diverse. It is currently the mainstream of the RWA market. It is still uncertain whether the RWA issued overseas by assets on the domestic consortium chain will be widely recognized and "re-financialized" in overseas DeFi and other fields. For Malu Grape RWA, whether it can be issued overseas or cooperate with traditional financial institutions in China for "re-financialization" (for example, it can be used as a refinancing collateral) remains to be explored.

Overall, RWA is still in its early stages of development, and the exploration of its business model has only just begun. This article does not touch on the more flexible and practical RWA, but is only a preliminary observation of the RWA business model. With the development of the market and the evolution of RWA, its business ecosystem will become more and more prosperous.

References:

Wang Yang, Bai Haifeng, Zhu Haokang, "RWA and Tokenization: Investment and Financing Transformation under Web3.0 Technology"

Citibank, "Money, Tokens and Games"

Shanghai Data Exchange, "Zuo'an Xinhui's First Launch of Malu Grape RWA, Exploring Innovative Applications of Data Elements"

Zero One Finance, "Mainland Assets Issue Tokens in Hong Kong, the Door Has Been Opened"

Zero One Finance, "Ant Digits Enters RWA"

These products and services obviously do not include those related to general market infrastructure such as cryptocurrency trading, custody, auditing and compliance. This report aims to highlight the early and major use cases of Ethereum used by traditional companies and institutions. Click to read

Nasdaq 100 Index stocks and Bitcoin are completely different but complementary investment portfolios. The Nasdaq 100 Index includes the largest non-financial stocks listed on the Nasdaq exchange with a concentration of technology stocks. Bitcoin is the first public blockchain and is now the largest crypto asset by market value. [1]Both the Nasdaq 100 and Bitcoin can be viewed as high-growth investments at the forefront of the digital transformation of the economy. Replacing some Nasdaq 100 stock investments with Bitcoin in a portfolio allows investors to reduce U.S. equity concentration risk and may help optimize risk-adjusted returns. Click to read

In CryptoDCA's recent article "What kind of mentality should we have before fixed investment", we realized that when implementing a fixed investment strategy, we must pay close attention to national policies and use these long-term and macro factors to guide our investment decisions. The impact of policy factors is often persistent and long-term. Taking a country's industrial development policy as an example, such policies usually take decades to show their effectiveness, and factors at this level of influence are usually easy for the public to understand, and their future trends can be predicted. Therefore, they can be an important reference for investors when considering market trends, especially for the prediction of long-term market trends. Click to read

4. Encrypted payment channel: a superconductor suitable for payment

data.title: Gold concept RWA is worth paying attention to, AI sector has a significant correction
data.description:
RWA concept is hot,while AI and DePIN suffered a major impact after the release of DeepSeek.

Crypto Market Summary

1. The RWA concept began to become popular, and FMG RWA Index YTD gained 8%;

2. Affected by TRUMP's bloodsucking and Deepseek, Web3 AI and DePIN sectors containing computing power have pulled back significantly;

3. The increase in the number of initial jobless claims in the United States provides room for the Federal Reserve to postpone interest rate cuts; in 2025, the integration of Web3 and traditional finance will be further deepened.

1. Market Overview

1.1 Analysis of FMG RWA and AI Index

In 2025, among the many indices monitored by FMG, RWA Index performed well, with a YTD return of 7.63%, which was mainly due to the strong rise of Mantra at the beginning of this year. Mantra is an RWA Layer1 that combines real-world assets. On January 9 this year, MANTRA announced that it had tokenized $1 billion of RWA assets for DAMAC, a UAE real estate company. DAMAC was previously mentioned by Trump at a press conference and will invest $20 billion in data centers in multiple states in the United States.

RWA expanded its crypto products around assets such as U.S. Treasuries and private credit in the early days, but is now gradually breaking away from the "constraints" of pure finance. The tokenization of commodities has not become an important trend in the RWA market. Real estate is a sector that is deeply integrated with RWA, and another important sector is gold. As one of the most stable assets in the world, the total market value of the current gold token market has reached $1.05 billion. FMG also attaches great importance to related projects, such as Paxos, Tether Holdings, Comtech Gold, etc.

In addition, public chains that issue RWA products have also become the focus of market attention, such as Mantra, which has performed well this year.

The AI ​​and DePIN Index have experienced a large correction, with a correction range of about 25%.

There are many reasons for this. First, starting from pump.fun, to the successive appearance of ai16z and Virtuals, the AI ​​sector has continued to be hot, but funds have not really flowed into large infrastructure and landing-level applications in the AI ​​field, but have been idling on Meme. But after TRUMP appeared, these idle funds were severely drained and plummeted across the board. Although the AI ​​sector recovered slightly after TRUMP fell, the launch of DeepSeek before the Spring Festival further hit the AI ​​sector. Its ARC market value fell from US$600 million to US$250 million, and Swamrs fell 90% from US$600 million to US$60 million.

If the previous popularity of AI Meme has hindered the market's attention to AI infrastructure and landing-level applications to a certain extent, then DeepSeek's open source and lower-cost advantages (under the premise that the product performance is similar to OpenAI, Deepseek's development cost through algorithm optimization is 5 million US dollars, which is one thousandth of OpenAI's 5 billion US dollars cost) has "subverted" the previous market's AI perception to a certain extent, directly leading to the decline of traditional AI stocks, and Web3 AI has not been spared. The impact on computing power-based AI and DePIN is even greater, and it can even be said to have shaken the foundation of the current Web3 AI to some extent.

1.2 Crypto Market Data

As of February 14, 2024, the total market value of cryptocurrency is 3.21 trillion, down 4.46% from 3.36 trillion in the first half of January.

BTC Dominance Index: As of February 14, the current BTC dominance index is 59.8%, up 4.1% from 55.7% in the first half of January.

Altcoin Season Arrival Index: As of February 14, the current Altcoin Season Arrival Index is 44, which means that the wealth effect is concentrating on BTC.

ETFIndex

BTC ETF: In the past 15 days, BTC ETF had a net outflow of US$154.6 million.

ETH ETF: In the past 15 days, ETH ETF had a net inflow of US$373 million.

1.3 CPI and other data and market reactions to market judgment

As of the week of February 1, 2025, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States increased by 11,000 to 219,000, higher than the market expectation of 213,000. Despite the increase, the figure is still at a historical low, indicating that the labor market remains strong. On January 30, the Federal Reserve FOMC meeting was held. At the meeting, the Federal Reserve decided to keep the federal funds rate unchanged at between 5.25% and 5.50%. This decision shows that the Federal Reserve remains concerned about inflation and employment in the US economy and maintains a cautious monetary policy stance.

On February 12 and 13, the United States released CPI and PPI data in succession. Among them, the increase in CPI in January exceeded expectations, which aggravated people's concerns about the continued existence of inflationary pressure and strengthened the Federal Reserve's cautious attitude in easing monetary policy. As for the PPI data, the increase of 0.4% was higher than the expectations of economists surveyed previously, but lower than the upwardly revised increase of 0.5% in December last year. Both data further narrowed the expectations of interest rate cuts, and risky assets will face uncertainty in the short term.

The impact of recent US economic data on the Web3 market is complex. Signs of slowing inflation may boost investor confidence and drive up cryptocurrency prices. However, solid employment and consumption data may make the Federal Reserve cautious on interest rate cuts, thereby limiting the upside of the cryptocurrency market. In addition, the Fed did not cut interest rates this time, which means that the financing cost is still high, which is not conducive to the rise of high-risk assets such as cryptocurrencies. The cryptocurrency market will bear downward pressure in the short term. It is worth noting that the financing difficulty of Web3 projects is still high due to the high interest rate environment, which makes venture capital more cautious. In the past year, many Web3 startups have scaled down or turned to other models such as RWA tokenization or AI+Web3 combination due to financing difficulties. If the market begins to expect interest rate cuts, it may attract VCs to pay attention to Web3 and the crypto market again.

2. Hot Market News

2.1 SoftBank promises to invest up to $40 billion in OpenAI and has the right to select other investors

SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are reaching a new investment plan: SoftBank will conduct an exclusive round of financing and promise to invest up to $40 billion in OpenAI. SoftBank is not expected to provide all the funds alone and has the right to invite other partners to participate in this round of financing. A person with direct knowledge of the plan said that OpenAI can approve or veto new investors. It is expected that this round of financing will value OpenAI at $260 billion.

2.2 Ripple continues to be optimistic about the market potential of RWA

Ripple believes that by 2033, the global custody service market size is expected to grow from US$41 billion in 2023 to more than US$100 billion. Among them, it is expected that by 2030, more than 10% of the world's assets will be tokenized, including RWA, such as financial instruments (stocks, bonds, ETFs) and even artworks and carbon credits. This wave of digital transformation that reshapes the custody landscape requires next-generation solutions. The development of RWA will also bring new use cases:

Issuing and custody of tokenized securities and RWA or cryptocurrency custody and trading wallets for institutional and retail investors;

Strong compliance controls and advanced governance framework to provide asset protection;

Becoming a sub-custodian on a global custodian network to enhance service distribution;

Seamlessly access liquidity providers, exchanges, and lending and staking platforms.

III. Regulatory Environment

3.1 SEC Cryptocurrency Working Group Work Plan

On February 4, 2025, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) released the Cryptocurrency Working Group's work plan, marking an important transformation in the U.S. cryptocurrency regulation. This working group aims to bring more clarity to the regulatory framework for crypto assets while maintaining support for innovation. Over the past decade, the SEC's performance in cryptocurrency regulation has often been criticized, mainly due to the lack of clear rules and uncertainty in enforcement. The newly established working group hopes to guide the cryptocurrency industry towards a more mature and regulated future in a more systematic and transparent manner.

The working group will work in multiple areas, such as: clarifying whether different types of cryptocurrencies are securities, which is the key to resolving other regulatory issues; providing legal certainty for cryptocurrency projects in specific circumstances through letters of no objection; exploring temporary exemptions for token issuance and simplifying the registration path (such as Regulation A and crowdfunding rules); providing compliant cryptocurrency custody solutions for investment advisors and brokers; clarifying whether crypto lending and pledging are subject to securities laws and formulating relevant rules.

3.2 Bipartisan resolution in the U.S. House of Representatives calls for clear cryptocurrency and blockchain regulations

Members of the U.S. House of Representatives introduced a resolution supporting blockchain technology and digital assets. The resolution highlights the economic potential of digital assets, the need for regulatory clarity, and the role of blockchain in improving transparency and efficiency. It also warns that without clear regulation, the United States could lose its innovation advantage to countries with clearer legal frameworks. Following the resolution, lawmakers issued a statement emphasizing the urgency of regulatory action.

data.title: Analysis of Four.Meme Security Incident How to Ensure BNB Chain Ecosystem Security
data.description:
On February 11, Four.Meme, the most popular memecoin launch platform of BNB Chain, was attacked and lost about $180,000. Previously, due to the popularity of Four.Meme's tutorial videos and the token $TST used for demonstration in the videos, Four.Meme attracted many users of BNB Chain, and the transactions of BNB Chain related to memecoin became more active.

Recently, there have been many attacks on Memecoin launch platforms. Although the amount of loss in this security incident is relatively small, it has once again sounded the alarm for security. AsBNB Chain's security partner, Beosin has previously completed the audit of ecological projects such as PancakeSwap. This article will analyze the Four.Meme security incident in detail for everyone to help users and developers understand the ecological security situation of BNB Chain.

Four.Meme Security Incident Analysis

Business Logic Introduction

Four.Meme is a memecoin launch platform similar to Pump.fun on BNB Chain. The characteristics of this type of launch platform are:

1. Provide users with a universal and customizable template for creating memecoin. Users only need to customize the name, logo, description and other information of Memecoin on the platform.

2. When the launch platform issues memecoin, the token will not be directly added to the liquidity pool on the decentralized exchange for trading. Instead, users must first pay SOL/BNB and other tokens to mint the issued memecoin. The token price during the minting process is determined by the Bonding Curve.

Beosin has previously made a detailed analysis of the business logic of Memecoin Launchpad. For more information, please read "Trump's coin issuance detonates Solana ecosystem. What security challenges does the Memecoin launch platform face?", which will not be repeated here.

For Four.Meme, when users continue to "buy" memecoin during the minting process, the price of memecoin will increase according to the joint curve it designed, and the corresponding market value will also increase. When the market value of memecoin issued by users reaches 24 BNB, the Four.Meme platform will migrate the remaining memecoin and 24 BNB to PancakeSwap (i.e., create a memecoin-BNB liquidity pool) for public trading.

The vulnerability of this security incident lies in the creation of the liquidity pool. The project party did not consider the situation where the liquidity pool was created in advance during the process of creating the liquidity pool.

Attack process

(1) The attacker first mints the token in the Four.meme contract before adding pancakeSwap liquidity to the token;

(2) Then create a trading pool of tokens and WBNB in ​​advance in PancakeV3Pool, and set an abnormally high token price;

(3) Then wait for the project to start normally. Since the trading pair has been created and initialized by the attacker before, PancakeV3Pool will add liquidity according to the price initialized by the attacker when it was created, which is equivalent to the initial price of the memecoin's public transaction being quietly raised by the attacker.

(4) After observing the addition of liquidity, the attacker immediately sold the previously minted tokens at a high price to make a profit.

https://bscscan.com/tx/0x2902f93a0e0e32893b6d5c907ee7bb5dabc459093efa6dbc6e6ba49f85c27f61

Taking the above attack transaction as an example, the attacker used only 1603 snowboard tokens to exchange for 23 BNB, taking away almost all the liquidity of BNB in ​​the liquidity pool.

According to Beosin KYT analysis, the flow of Four.Meme's stolen funds is shown in the figure below:

Currently, the stolen funds have been transferred from the address 0x482b004e7800174a1eFb87f496552ac8F53B2fda to the address 0xfD3772890EcF7b514a09dD602e78eAb35A4298E1.

Hidden dangers under the memecoin craze

With CZ's call and promotion on social media about the BNB Chain ecosystem memecoin, BNB Chain began to take over Solana's memecoin popularity, and trading volume and user activity increased significantly. $TST, a test token used by the BNB Chain team for video teaching, was launched on Binance, with a market value of up to $500 million.

In the early morning of February 14th, Beijing time, CZ announced that his pet dog's name was Broccoli, which triggered the memecoin war in BNB Chain, and countless memecoins named Broccoli appeared on the chain. It should be noted that CZ reiterated that he would not issue memecoins. These memecoins named Broccoli were just riding on CZ's popularity, and the token prices fluctuated violently.

According to Beosin Alert, there have been 6 Rug Pull incidents related to Broccoli memecoin so far. The following is one of the Rug Pull incidents, after the token issuer removed liquidity, the token price plummeted 99.94%, making a profit of about $100,000:

Rug Pull of a Broccoli Token

To avoid asset loss, users need to understand at least the following 4 points when trading memecoin:

1. Platform risks of Memecoin Launchpad

2. Risks of Memecoin imitations/Pixiu disks with the same name

3. Whether Memecoin can be issued in excess
